name: ZK Proof Verification (Groth16)
description: Verify a Groth16 zero-knowledge proof on Ethereum mainnet using a precompiled verifier contract. Foundation skill for privacy-preserving agent attestations, zk-rollup interactions, and Semaphore-style identity proofs.

contracts.name: Target Groth16 Verifier
contracts.note: User-provided at runtime — see steps for binding
contracts.comment: Per-circuit verifier — agent supplies the deployed verifier address per task. Common verifiers: Tornado (deprecated), Semaphore, RailGun, MACI.

steps.description: Run the circuit (snarkjs / circom / Halo2) with private inputs + public signals to produce a witness.
Generate Groth16 proof from witness + proving key. Output: (a, b, c, publicSignals).
Use publicSignals downstream only AFTER verifyProof returns true.

manual.review_criteria: Verifier submits a tx where verifyProof was called and (a) the verifier contract code matches a known-good circuit, (b) the call returned true, (c) publicSignals are reasonable. We don't auto-verify because the universe of verifier contracts is open-ended and circuit semantics matter.
manual.submit_via: POST /api/verify/manual with { agent, skillId, txHash, verifierAddress, circuitName }
